rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUE HONE LEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney on 2/28/25 with the following issues: 1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. Please revise Item 8 of the Petition for Letters of Administration. 2) If an heir or devisee died after the decedent, the petitioner must allege whether or not a personal representative has been appointed for such deceased heir or devisee. If no personal representative has been appointed for the post-deceased heir or devisee, or such personal representative is the same person as the petitioner, all heirs and/or devisees of the post-deceased heir or devisee must be listed. Whether all parties noticed will be reviewed once addressed. 3) Waiver of bond has not been filed by all heirs. The waivers of bond attached to the petition are also incomplete (name of proposed personal representative), and new waivers must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARILYN ANN MALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled notice in a contested matter. Proof of service must be filed as a separate document (not as an attachment to the proposed order merely lodged). See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 2) Notice must be given to the surety if the accounting is objected to, as the surety remains liable for the administrator's acts and omissions predating the bond exoneration in March 2023. See Probate Code section 1213(a)(2). 3) The objector requests that her 11/06/2023 fee petition be put back on calendar, but the petition has not been refiled as a new petition since the Court took it off calendar at the 07/09/2024 hearing, and it is unclear whether the other parties stipulate to an amendment or supplement of the objection to add the objector's extraordinary fees ($300,486) and costs ($8,812) sought by her 11/06/2023 petition. 4) The statutory fees payable to both attorneys for their ordinary services appear to be $240 based on the inventory and appraisal as corrected. It is unclear whether the objector, as the administrator's former attorney, intends to request her share of the statutory fees. Although the estate is insolvent and has no assets on hand, the decedent's trust is liable for unsatisifed expenses of administration of this probate, including the objector's fees and costs approved by the Court. See section 19001(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD BLAIR FLORENCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney on 2/28/25 with the following issues: 1) Over $422K is in one bank. Please provide proof that it is fully insured (e.g. it is a sweep account) or fully insure the funds and provide proof of full insurance. 2) Petitioner states that all beneficiaries are in agreement as to all fees and costs. Please file consents, which should be specific (e.g. specific fees and costs of litigation). 3) Timesheets for attorney services must be provided: a. Separate timesheet for will litigation (prior to appointment) b. Separate timesheet for probate administration. If extraordinary fees are being requested, separate timesheets must be provided for ordinary services and extraordinary services.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TOM TIEU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues on the amended petition include: a. The amended petition only addresses 2023 income taxes. Petitioner must allege that all income taxes due or payable by the estate have been paid / secured. b. Petitioner alleges in Par. 11 that TD Ameritrade Account 1344 was not liquidated, and the investment was transferred to the estate investment account at Charles Schwab; however, the revised assets on hand schedule does not show any non-cash assets. c. Paragraph 7 of the amended petition is incomplete (stops mid-sentence). No justification has been provided to not appraise the assets at date of death and by the probate referee. d. Notice of Hearing filed on 2/3/25 provides a 1-day notice of the hearing on 2/4/25. Notice of Hearing must be given and filed again. e. A revised proposed order should be filed if any changes will be made. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Order Compelling Accounting And For Order For Turnover Of Trust Funds By Former Trustee Charlene Monroe
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A).2) Notice is insufficient. 15 days' notice was given to the trust beneficiaries, but trust matters require 30 days' notice (Probate Code Section 17203). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879.
